sources: packet brief or explicit task scope
required_segments.purpose: Carry the execution boundary and narrowed local rules before broader repo context enters the packet.
Preserve accepted truth precedence and keep provisional material non-default.
Map the packet to accepted workspace families and current materialization state.

sources: local docs nearest the touched files
required_segments.purpose: Preserve the accepted local-doc-first read order.

required_segments.included_when: when module boundaries, validation, proof, or fixture expectations materially apply
required_segments.purpose: Carry boundary and proof/fixture discipline into later implementation-facing packets.

required_segments.included_when: only after local docs leave a bounded unresolved question and the selected budget band allows it
sources: supporting repo reads named in packet notes
required_segments.purpose: Keep supporting reads additive rather than the default mode.

required_segments.included_when: only when a bounded read-only subagent was allowed by the selected budget band
sources: read-only subagent summary attached back to the primary packet owner
required_segments.purpose: Carry bounded exploration results without replacing the owner's required reads.

file_scope_rules: Prefer explicit file_targets or file_whitelist_ref from the packet brief.
Use the workspace manifest index and current-state view to map file scope to accepted root families and current materialization state.
If required files fall outside the named scope or outside the selected budget band, split or escalate rather than silently widening the packet.
Cross-root edits are allowed only when every touched root is explicitly named in the packet scope or task.
hard_exclusions: Non-accepted artifacts as default truth unless the packet explicitly targets them.
Transcript continuity or ambient provider state as sufficient replay basis.
Console-only or log-only proof, delta, review, or approval state.
Semantic ownership inferred from file adjacency alone.
Direct UI-to-worker or app-to-gateway shortcuts that bypass typed control contracts.
output_requirements: List governing accepted artifact refs and any materially relevant source-authority refs.
List file_targets or file_whitelist_ref and touched roots.
Name module-boundary contracts and fixture rules when they materially apply.
Record supporting reads or bounded subagent findings only when used.
State the split-or-escalate reason when a band breach occurs.
